item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Keep reading, futuranth. I'll be here when you go."

nervous breathing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, So this scp still struggle people to find all of its iterations, So I will help with that.

Go to tag search select fragment then search by date created ascending, It's should appear as the first five results.

Your welcome :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is one of my all time favourite SCPs. I love a good format-screw. I especially enjoyed the hints that the Foundation doesn't know the full extent of 1893's abilities, the name-inserts in particular being a great touch. I also really like the varied tones of the iterations- Iteration D is probably my personal favourite.

My only complaint is that it takes a very long time to read all of the iterations, due to the randomised nature of the page & the fact the links on the discussion pages no longer work.

item.title: Like mine...
item.content:encoded:
I know this is an old post, but I know exactly what you mean.

Mine is Skittlebunny. Seeing that appear in articles is 100% the opposite of jarring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first couple times I read this, I did so while not logged in. I loved it and upvoted. Now I'm finally reading it while logged in and…. wow. The extra lines are terrifying. This is the first time I've been legitimately creeped out by that type of format trick. If I could upvote again I would. This is probably my favourite SCP. Excellent job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"he bled so much cbriggs i wish you had been there i wish you had seen it i wish it was you"
Ok, I know that this is just a formatting trick, but everything about this is horrifying. Usually when stuff like this uses my name it doesn't scare me, but the implications of the article itself knowing who I am and talking directly to me actually freaked me out a bit. Also one issue I have with this article is that I have a hard time understanding what it actually does when it attacks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i like this gimmick, it's very clever, but what does the scp actually do other than affect stories about it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Welp, I apparently got B right off the bat despite everyone here saying it takes forever to get it.

Also, and then F came out. Do you think god never comes down because he's scared of what he created?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Burly dude with horn tattoos on his head? Immediately made me think of Alex Terrible from Slaughter to Prevail.

All that aside, this is fucking awesome. I'm having trouble finding version A and B, but the other 3 that I managed to get were amazing. Maybe I'll come back later and try again.

EDIT: Found A. Waiting on B still.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there actually an iteration E, or is that just a goof that went over my head? I've been checking this page for an hour, and I only get a-d. Absolutely love the idea of the minotaur's presence as a danger indicator in the stories. I got D first, so it seemed pretty innocuous, but when I finally got C, my heart got to pumping pretty hard. These meta-tricks are so worthwhile when done properly, and this is maybe the perfect example of using them well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As soon as I post this, it reloads to Iteration D. This article is magic

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Where’s Iteration D?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first it seems pretty benign. okay, it's in form of a story, it changes every 60 seconds, it uses my name. sounds like run-of-the-mill format screw.

and then I actually read what's different between iterations, and the creep hits me like a cold Monday shower. Deeper than I thought. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AKA "How to include LGBT characters the right way"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The moment i saw my username i was like "WiLl LoOkS lIkE sHiT jUsT gOt SeRiOus"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing scared me more than seeing my own username.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So good. The best part was my username appearing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would have upvoted anyway, but upon getting to lesbians in a non-sexual tragic love story, I had to upvote immediately before continuing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Am I doing something wrong?

Nope; those links are old and no longer functioning. I believe the individual iteration pages were ported over to different urls for better navigation.

Besides, that comment from TroyL is over 5 years old.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The links that you (TroyL) and Communism will win provided do not appear to be working. It just keeps sending me to a page much like those that appear when you click on an SCP slot that has not been used yet.
Am I doing something wrong?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I need to remove something from my history now.

Good, now if only I had some amenestics.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The many stories, the use of the person's name, the baseball bat, the entire thing, it is all done perfectly.
+1, no, +10? no, +100, if only I could do that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How we all wish we could…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't forget good ole Iteration F

item.title: Re: εїз
item.content:encoded: Yaaaaayyyy! (ﾉ◕ヮ◕)ﾉ*:・ﾟ✧*:・ﾟ✧*:・ﾟ✧

item.title: Re: εїз
item.content:encoded: I'm happy to say that all Narrative tag articles are correctly appearing in the tag search.

item.title: εїз
item.content:encoded: εїз εїз εїз

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i should have friggin known this would be the first narrative tag feature, how did i not see this coming? anyways, very well done article with a unique format screw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. I did not know that there was a username module. Welp, time to reread every iteration.

A task made much harder by the iteration links on the first page no longer working.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Recently, I heard of a Japanese ghost story called Cow Head. It is supposedly so scary that one can be scared to death. It is also said that the story was cut into pieces and distributed as separate things.

And minotaurs have cow (bull)1 heads, yes? And this story is presented in pieces, but never as a true whole, yes?

Footnotes
1. The kanji does not specify the gender.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, my understanding is that this minotaur is THE Minotaur. The one from Greek legend. And that these stories are actually the labyrinth. That basically no normal means of containment could hold the minotaur. And I'm guessing the person who talks to you "like this" could be Daedalus or Icarus.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's startling to see this with my forum username in this.

God help those poor sods who used their facebook accounts to log in.

1+. Teach me your ways, oh master of code and making things creepy on a personal level.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The name thing startles me every time. How did you do this? I looked at the code but it didn't help.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was on board until the third story I got to.

My family had a dog named Sophie who had to get put down at the vet when she was old. Creepy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first time I read these, I wasn't a member yet, so I missed the whole user-name thing. I was just going back through all the top-rated SCPs — which made this rather startling, eep.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Years late to the party, here, but that's exactly how I wound up doing it (purely on accident after realizing I wasn't logged in), and boy howdy, disturbed is the right word all right.

+1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So yeah, this is pretty great, but just had a fridge logic moment: if this can only be perceived through text referring to itself, how was it discovered in the first place?
Nothin' like a good format screw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so creepy and great and you should definitely make a Leak post about it because there's so much more I want to know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really dislike yournamehere scp's, so i cant upvote it, but reading it while not logged in has prevented me from downvoting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great. I understand that interface screws and gimmicks might have gotten more prevalent, but this is still legitimately creepy and great.

I'm also wondering if this takes inspiration from House of Leaves? A really great novel for anyone who hasn't read it. It's pretty much an SCP in book format and length. (Including the interface screws in book form.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"You don't seem to understand, Macleod." The voice came from everywhere and nowhere, as the Agent turned around to face the indescribably horrific creature. As its entire mass writhed as if in agony, it continued its speech. "I was going to post with the format screw, but I could never find the time to do it." As a spike of flesh surged out, impaling MacLeod through the chest, a figure burst through the wall, smacking the terror around with a blood-soaked baseball bat. As the life faded from the monstrosity, the figure, now visible as being the minotaur, walked up to MacLeod with a warm smile on his face. "Thank you for giving me the opportunity to do so." The Agent's eyes widened in horror as the friendly face shifted into a devilish grin, and he barely had time to scream before his skull caved in from the blunt force of the bat.

(The message for lazy people)

You don't seem to understand, Macleod. I was going to post in the format screw, but I could never find the time to do it. Thank you for giving me the opportunity to do so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Not gonna lie, I think this minotaur thing is all type and no do. Like, I bet I could take him." That's what I heard MacLeod say, shortly before we encountered the beast, before the man came after us.

"Anyways, +1." That's what I heard him scream as the thing tore him apart. As I was running away, I tripped over a baseball bat. I tried to run away, but it was already too late…

Seriously, no format screws in the discussion? You guys were all on top of that shit for the damn toaster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1-year necro, but…

+ show block
– hide block

All clowns are the minotaur.
– hide block

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
And the point of that comment would be?

santenoturtle11, when you comment on an article, try to make sure there's actually some meaningful content in there. This is what we call shitposting. Please don't do it again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't fuck with me, SCP- D.O.U.C.H.E.F.A.C.E.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Scrolling down to see how long SCP this is before starting to read it, then my name pops up in middle of text and my face o_o followed by heart attack x_x … and now that I´ve finished reading it, I gotta say great SCP! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are two thoughts I had regarding this.

  1. its scp-1893 reacting to a [DATA EXPUNGED]
  2. 1893 can change content, removed this section and lied about not being able to change the content.

I wasn't that keen on this until I refreshed the page, then the concept made sense. upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very good stuff. When I first realized the article's format and started reading, I thought to myself "another meta-SCP?! Come on guys, it was fun the first time someone did this but it worked because no-one else had done it before. Now it's just looking uninspired" but as I read on, it won me over more and more.

It is very well written in a genuinely disturbing style, even the lighter versions, and while I kind of saw the "enter your username here" thing coming from other similar article, it still made me feel uneasy when it actually happened.

I also especially like the fact that it tells just enough to keep us interested but leaves enough to the imagination to make us want to know more.

Only one thing I find strange, the description states that the original text remains unchanged and present in its entirety, which is true for most of it, but the end of the addendum is always cut off. Doesn't that contradict the SCP's effects?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1 for the author!
The first hit was seeing my username.
The second was that, in Iteration B, it stated that the ringtone was a Wu-Tang Clan song.
My ringtone is a Wu-Tang Clan song, "Bring Da Ruckus".

I really like the SCPs that take advantage of the medium their written in.
On a side note, was the Wu-Tang thing just a coincidence?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice SCP, but my name sorta ruined it for me…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: GG, Esko. Seems like you're the best at the whole format screw thing. Not tremendously keen on the username inclusion - feels a bit too commonly used these days - but overall very good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn it. I was just about to close the wiki and drift off to sleep, and now I need to go buy a nightlight.

Night light...

Oh, hell!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of wish my username weren't so silly for stuff like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't get it.

REREAD: I get it. No wait, I don't.

REREREAD: I get it, but I don't get what it is.

REREREREAD: I am the minotaur. All clowns are the minotaur. All clowns are the minotaur. It will never be your birthday again.

REREREREREAD: I'll fuck with whoever I want to, asshole. You don't scare me, narrative framed around an SCP!

REREREREREREREAD: Think I've fully gotten it now. But what is it? It's essentially a description of itself, changed in framing by unknown force, potentially itself? I got it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ask the entity if it knows anything about Fred.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I keep getting Iteration B. I think it likes me guys!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is amazing, and seeing my name gave me chills
++++++1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to yawn-and-pass because I've been getting complacent as of late, and then BAM username. I gave it its 299th bump, someone needs to give it the 300th. It deserves it. Side note, if you are actually a paranoid person in meatspace, use only a facebook (or similar) with your real name.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just read this for the first time since getting a login and HOLY HELL.
